behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: a pure mock can miss real filename discovery/sorting edge cases and DB write ordering. REAL-INFRA: this depends on real filesystem enumeration plus persistence of applied rows. No LLM or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp `migrations/` directory with out-of-order files (e.g., `010_b.sql`, `002_a.sql`, `100_c.sql`), run the runner against a real local Postgres test DB, then assert side-effect tables/rows show execution in numeric prefix order and `_migrations` records the same sequence.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: idempotency bugs often appear in DB state checks (`_migrations`) and real SQL side effects, which mocks can hide. REAL-INFRA: needs real DB state across two runs, but not OS/process orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run runner once with a migration that inserts a sentinel row and records in `_migrations`; run runner again against same DB; assert sentinel effect count stays 1 and `_migrations` has no duplicate record for that version.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: transaction rollback semantics and stop-on-failure behavior are easy to fake incorrectly with mocks. REAL-INFRA: requires real DB transaction behavior, but can be exercised in one process without OS-level coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare migration A with multiple statements where one fails after an earlier write, plus migration B that would write a distinct marker. Run runner on real Postgres; assert A’s partial writes are absent (rolled back), B was not applied, and only pre-failure successful prior migrations (if any) remain recorded.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: correctness boundary includes true cross-process concurrency/races (separate processes, timing, locking/constraints). MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks or single-process tests can miss inter-process race conditions. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn two runner subprocesses simultaneously against the same Postgres DB and same migration set; wait for both to exit; assert each migration’s side effect exists exactly once and `_migrations` contains one row per migration version (no duplicates, no misses). Repeat several times to increase race detection.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: bootstrap-order failures involve real DB DDL + first migration execution ordering; mocks can incorrectly pass. REAL-INFRA: needs fresh DB and real schema inspection, but no subprocess requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Against a fresh Postgres DB with no `_migrations` table, run runner with at least one migration. Assert run succeeds, `_migrations` exists, first migration is applied, and metadata row insertion is valid for that first migration (proving table creation happened before recording/application flow required it).
